title: Deliveries, automation, and the gateway drug to electrification with BIG JOE
summary: Full autonomy, big delivery numbers, and a push towards electrification that’s moving global markets? We must be talking about electric forklifts! On today’s uplifting episode of Quick Charge, we’ve got Big Joe CMO Martin Boyd here to walk us through some of the ways these battery powered equipment assets are making all our lives better.

title: Tesla still has no real plan for Hardware 3 owners promised FSD
summary:
Elon Musk was asked directly on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call whether the company still plans to upgrade Hardware 3 (HW3) cars so they can run its latest “Full Self-Driving” software. His answer made one thing clear: Tesla still has no concrete plan.

Instead of committing to a fix, Musk floated a grab-bag of options and timelines — none of them firm — for the millions of vehicles Tesla sold with the promise that they had “all the hardware necessary” for self-driving.

title: Elon Musk hints at Tesla (TSLA)-SpaceX merger on earnings call
summary:
Elon Musk hinted at a merger between Tesla and SpaceX on Tesla’s Q2 2026 earnings call, pointing to “more and more overlap” between the two companies he controls.

He stopped short of confirming anything, deferring to Tesla’s general counsel and “the appropriate process” — but the exchange is the closest Musk has come to addressing the merger speculation directly.

title: Tesla says ‘Robotaxi’ is expanding — its own chart shows it’s not
summary:
Tesla told shareholders this week that its Robotaxi service is expanding. The company’s own chart proves it isn’t.

In its Q2 2026 update, Tesla leaned on a cumulative “paid Robotaxi miles” chart climbing past 2.4 million miles as its headline proof of momentum. But break that curve into quarters and the growth flatlines: Robotaxi added roughly 900,000 paid miles in Q2 2026, the same as it did in Q1.

title: Subaru is delaying the 3-row Getaway EV, following Toyota
summary: Subaru confirmed that production of the Getaway EV is being delayed, following Toyota’s decision to delay its first three-row electric SUV.

title: Tesla (TSLA) releases Q2 2026 financial results: record revenue, big profit miss
summary:
Tesla (TSLA) released its financial results and shareholders’ letter for the second quarter (Q2) 2026 after market close today. We are updating this post with all the details from the financial results, shareholders’ letter, and the conference call later tonight.

The earnings call with management is scheduled for 5:30 p.m. ET.

title: Ford F-150 electric trucks can now power a home’s electrical panel
summary: Ford F-150 Lightning or hybrid owners can now use their trucks to keep hard-wired home essentials running during an outage by connecting their truck directly to their home’s main electrical panel. And even better, it’s affordable.

title: France won’t approve Tesla ‘FSD’ yet, wants EU-wide sign-off first
summary:
France’s Transport Minister, Philippe Tabarot, says the country won’t authorize Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” (Supervised) “as it currently stands,” pointing to two safety problems: the system lets drivers speed, and it doesn’t keep them attentive enough in cities.

But this isn’t France slamming the door on Tesla. It’s France refusing to rubber-stamp the Netherlands’ national approval and holding out for a proper EU-wide sign-off.

title: Anker SOLIX Summer Camp power station sale with exclusive savings from $189, HiEHA CarPlay SU8 2.0 e-bike low, EcoFlow, more
summary:
At the front of our midweek Green Deal’s pack is the current Anker SOLIX Summer Camp Outlet Sale with up to $1,496 savings on power stations + exclusive bonus savings – all starting from $189. Right behind it is HiEHA’s CarPlay-equipped SU8 2.0 Smart Moped-Style e-bike down at a $750 low, alongside a dual-battery bundle option and the bike’s first-generation predecessor. From there, we have EcoFlow’s 5-Day Member Flash Sale with six different offers (including an EcoCredits promotion) starting from $1, the Segway Navimow i105N and i110N Robot Lawn Mowers starting from $669, and much more. As always, you can find all the hangover deals from this week down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s launch deal on the new roborock RockMow X120H LiDAR AWD Robot Lawn Mower, the soon-to-end Bluetti July Member Day power station sale lineup, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Toyota’s next-gen Corolla EV takes center stage as a ‘sense of crisis’ reshapes the company
summary: The world’s best-selling car of all time is going electric. Toyota’s next-generation Corolla EV will debut on a new platform as “a considerable sense of crisis” is forcing the company to reinvent itself.

title: Ford Just Made It Easier To Back Up Your House With An F-150 Hybrid Or Lightning
summary: The new solution means you don't need to use a mess of extension cords to back up your fridge or other appliances.

title: Tesla Is Recalling 15 Cars For Being Too Canadian
summary: Tesla accidentally sold 15 customers cars that it imported from Canada, which means they don't meet federal safety standards.

title: The Tesla Diner Was The World's Most Utilized Supercharger In Its First Year
summary: The Tesla Diner delivered more electricity than any other Supercharger station in the world, averaging 1,600 sessions per day.

title: BYD Wants To Take the World's Fastest Car Record... From Itself
summary: BYD's own Yangwang U9 Xtreme already holds the production car top speed record, but it's determined to beat that soon.

title: EVs Just Hit 25% Market Share In Europe
summary: Electric cars accounted for 25.6% of June registrations across 17 European markets, as sales jumped nearly 40%.

title: Tesla Has To Fix Its Super-Bright Headlights. A New Law Could Change Things For Good.
summary: Tesla called its uber-bright headlights "inconsequential" to safety. Now the feds are making it fix the problem.

title: Used EV Values Are Actually Rising. Why That's Good And Bad News.
summary: Used cars don't usually become more valuable over time—and especially not used electric ones.

title: Polestar Won't Appeal Its U.S. Ban. Now Dealers Are Left Holding The Bag
summary: The company had “significant dialogue” with American authorities and believes an appeal would lead nowhere.

title: PSA: Don't Use An Old Charging Adapter For Your Rivian R2. It May Melt
summary: Since Rivian stuck with 400 volts to keep costs down, the R2's high-amperage charging can be too much for some older adapters.

title: Volkswagen's E-Bike Tries To Keep You Safe With A Rearview Camera And Smart Helmet
summary: The Smart Helmet pairs with Smart Glasses to give the rider live navigation, traffic, and safety alerts.
